minutes_briefs.title: Serious illness or injury leave rules take effect April 1 in BC
minutes_briefs.summary: A new BC law gives workers the right to take unpaid leave if they or a family member has a serious illness or injury. The change takes effect on April 1, 2026. Employers must allow this leave and cannot punish workers for taking it.
minutes_briefs.action_required: Update your employee handbook and payroll procedures to include the new serious illness or injury leave entitlement.

key_dates.label: BC serious illness or injury leave rules take effect

minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 34/2026
Made under: Employment Standards (Serious Illness Or Injury Leave) Amendment Act
Published in: No. 5 – B.C. Regs. 29 to 42, March 24, 2026
Deposit date: March 16, 2026

B.C. Reg. 34/2026, deposited March 16, 2026, under theEMPLOYMENT STANDARDS (SERIOUS ILLNESS OR INJURY LEAVE) AMENDMENT ACT, 2025[section 5]. Order in Council 79/2026, approved and ordered March 16, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that, effective April 1, 2026, section 2 of theEmployment Standards (Serious Illness or Injury Leave) Amendment Act, 2025, S.B.C. 2025, c. 27, is brought into force. — J. WHITESIDE,Minister of Labour; A. DIX,Presiding Member of the Executive Council.
